title: Share of women in ministerial positions
subtitle: Deputy prime ministers and prime ministers or heads of government that hold ministerial portfolios are included, vice-presidents and heads of governmental or public agencies are excluded.
caption:
Share of women in ministerial positions

Deputy prime ministers and prime ministers or heads of government that hold ministerial portfolios are included, vice-presidents and heads of governmental or public agencies are excluded.

This choropleth world map displays the share of women in ministerial positions for the year 2024. The legend uses a color scale ranging from light beige for 0% to dark purple for values greater than 50%, with intermediate bands at 0-10%, 10-20%, 20-30%, 30-40%, and 40-50%. The darkest purple shade, representing a share above 50%, is visible in a small number of countries, including Spain, Finland, and Nicaragua. Several nations in the Americas and Europe, such as Canada, Mexico, France, and Australia, appear in the 30-40% or 40-50% bands, shown in medium to dark purple tones. A large portion of the map, covering much of Africa, Asia, and parts of Europe, is colored in lighter shades of pink and beige, indicating shares generally falling between 0% and 20%. Specific countries like Saudi Arabia and Yemen sit in the lightest beige band, corresponding to the 0-10% range. Greenland is marked with diagonal hatching, indicating no data is available for that territory. The visual distribution shows higher concentrations of women in ministerial roles in specific pockets of Europe, North America, and Latin America, while lower shares are widespread across the Middle East, Asia, and Africa.
